title: Graphardisia lilacina
intro: Graphardisia lilacina, also known as the purple guinea gold, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, belonging to the family Annonaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and its extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Graphardisia lilacina include annonaceous acetogenins, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiparasitic activities. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to promote health and well-being, although its use should be carefully monitored due to potential toxicity.
